topic: Parallax = firmament wobble — c8 verification of ISS-1998
our_argument_summary: WIN-017 (verdict: Refuted by Data). A rigid 20m firmament wobble at H=9,086 km would produce one fixed angular displacement (≈0.454 arcsec) for all stars, but Gaia DR3 measurements of 1.8 billion stars show parallax inversely proportional to distance. Independent cross-checks (Cepheid/RR-Lyrae photometric, spectroscopic-class) all show the same 1/d scaling, and the NASA New Horizons spacecraft-baseline parallax experiment (32.4 arcsec Proxima Centauri, 15.7 arcsec Wolf 359) provides a geometry-only cross-check that the dome model cannot accommodate.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-017 appears only in the dome WIN registry; there is no live fetch and no comparison against any Gaia value in the monitor. The 0.454 arcsec figure for a 20m / 9,086 km wobble is algebraically derivable but is itself fit to the published Gaia range.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No automation — the WIN is a static registry entry.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The 20m amplitude is the value required to match observed parallax magnitudes via arctan(amp/H); no independent measurement constrains it. A non-rigid or variable-amplitude wobble would be needed to mimic 1/d scaling, which the review flags as an unfalsifiable free parameter.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No domain in monitor.py; no fetch call for parallax data.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard astronomy predicts parallax inversely proportional to distance (heliocentric model). The dome predicts a fixed displacement (firmament wobble). These are different numerical predictions, so this is not a relabeling — it is a substantive disagreement that observation resolves against the dome.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Heliocentric parallax has been published since 1838 (Bessel, 61 Cygni). Gaia DR3 (2023) is decades after the dome model existed. The dome's "prediction" cites known measurements.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The 0.454 arcsec figure follows from arctan(20m / 9,086km) — but the 20m amplitude is fit, not derived. No first-principles dome derivation produces 20m.

change_summary: Verification cycle for ISS-1998 (decider self-apply): the spectroscopic-class sentence in detail_evidence was split. Previously a single 99-word sentence containing a 73-word parenthetical; now two sentences (~30 words + ~62 words) with the Hyades-anchor clause as standalone follow-up. The split also strengthened the prose from "shows the same 1/d scaling, confirming..." (passive) to "the dome wobble predicts flat amplitude regardless of which rung the calibration sits on — so the 1/d scaling falsifies the wobble at every rung" (active falsification). Decider commit landed via FUSE → workspace-sync universal-pusher (a19e3115) at 2026-05-13T02:05:57Z.

iss_1998_verification.verification_pattern: git show origin/main:data/wins.json | grep -qF 'A second independence check: cross-validation against spectroscopic-class distance estimates, which set'
iss_1998_verification.note_on_decider_failure: Same race condition as ISS-1997: closed-issues.json reports verify_pattern_failed at 2026-05-13T02:05:50Z; the universal-pusher rescue committed at 02:05:57Z, 7 seconds later. Pattern PASSES against current origin/main.
iss_1998_verification.structural_check: The decider's split produced a stronger argument than the ISS-1998 description literally suggested. ISS-1998 proposed two sentences whose second sentence ended with a Gaia-DR3 dual-independence closer. The decider instead used the second sentence to assert active falsification ("the 1/d scaling falsifies the wobble at every rung"). This is preferable rhetorically — it converts the Hyades-anchor concession from a hedge into a defensive moat. Approved.

holes_found.description: Same pipeline-bookkeeping note as WIN-028 c9: ISS-1998 remains in closed-issues.json with status "fixed-pending-verification" and a stale "verification_pattern_failed" reason. The pattern actually passes now. The next decider verify-pending sweep should auto-flip; flagging for awareness, not for patch.
holes_found.recommendation: No prose action required. If still stuck after the next decider run, route to tinker for verify-pending-vs-workspace-sync timing audit. The 7-second gap between verify_pending_run (02:05:50Z) and the universal-pusher rescue commit (02:05:57Z) is exactly the kind of cross-process race that a defensive retry would close.

holes_found.description: Word-count check on the (now-split) spectroscopic-class section: first sentence is ~30 words, second is ~62 words. The second sentence is still long and dense ("Even granting that spectroscopic M_V calibrations carry residual parallax dependence at the Hyades-anchored level, the dome wobble predicts flat amplitude regardless of which rung the calibration sits on — so the 1/d scaling falsifies the wobble at every rung, and the spectroscopic-class data confirms the 1/d pattern is in the geometry, not in the photometric calibration chain.") and uses a 16-word em-dash clause. However, it now contains the active-falsification core of the argument and the dependent clauses sit at sentence-end (good reading order), so the read-rate cost is much lower than the prior 99-word sentence-with-parenthetical. Not worth further splitting at this point. Recording the new fingerprint so future drift-audits flag any re-bloating.
holes_found.recommendation: No action. The new fingerprint (detail_evidence length 2201, was longer before reordering) is recorded.

holes_found.description: Carry-over verification (not a new hole): the c7-H4 carry-over note flagged that ISS-1849/ISS-1854 (rhetorical-polish edits to detail_evidence) were closed wontfix. That disposition is still in force and reflected in closed-issues.json. The current detail_evidence remains consistent with the wontfix rationale ("pure rhetoric polish, no substantive credibility impact"). No re-action needed.

stronger_arguments.description: Optional future enhancement (NOT a current hole): the New Horizons sentence in detail_evidence (data/wins.json:WIN-017.detail_evidence after "Spacecraft-based measurements") could be tightened by noting that NH's 7 billion km baseline is 770,000× the dome firmament height — this number is already in the text. A minor framing improvement would be to lead with the ratio rather than the absolute distance, since "770,000× the firmament height" is more rhetorically powerful than "7 billion km from Earth". Marking as stronger-argument for future analyst consideration, not current cycle work.

kernel_of_truth.description: The ISS-1998 patch addresses a real readability defect in the c7 review (WIN-017.c7.json:holes_found[3]). The original 99-word sentence with 73-word parenthetical placed the dome-falsification logic inside a parenthetical, which is the wrong structural slot for the core argument. The decider's patch (data/wins.json:WIN-017.detail_evidence, sentence beginning "Even granting that spectroscopic M_V...") elevates the falsification claim to a top-level sentence, which is exactly what the c7-H3 recommendation pushed for.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The dome's underlying claim (parallax = firmament wobble) still fails at three levels in the same detail_evidence (data/wins.json:WIN-017.detail_evidence): (a) Gaia DR3 1.8B stars show parallax ∝ 1/distance, which a rigid wobble cannot reproduce; (b) the photometric independence check rules out a calibration-chain artifact; (c) the New Horizons spacecraft-baseline parallax (Throop et al. 2020 RNAAS 4, 74) directly measures stars at 7 billion km baseline — 770,000× any firmament height the dome could publish. Sentence-split prose polish does not change any of this; it only makes the photometric-independence sub-argument easier to parse.

deeper_analysis: Adversarial steelman: a dome defender could argue "the spectroscopic-class independence check is moot because all stellar M_V calibrations ultimately anchor to parallax through the Hyades cluster, so this isn't actually an independent check." The patched prose explicitly grants this point ("Even granting that spectroscopic M_V calibrations carry residual parallax dependence at the Hyades-anchored level") and then defuses it: a rigid wobble predicts a flat amplitude regardless of which calibration rung is used, so observing 1/d scaling falsifies the wobble at every rung. This is the correct rebuttal — it doesn't require the spectroscopic check to be fully calibration-independent; it only requires the 1/d signature to show up in the data, which it does at every rung. The defender attack fails. The New Horizons spacecraft-baseline cross-check (data/wins.json:WIN-017.detail_evidence "Spacecraft-based measurements" paragraph) provides the geometric-only nail in the coffin: the wobble model predicts no parallax for stars beyond firmament height, but NH measured distance-dependent parallax from a baseline 770,000× the firmament height. No calibration argument applies to NH because no photometric ladder is involved.

citations_verified: Gaia Collaboration 2023 A&A 674 A1 (doi.org/10.1051/0004-6361/202243940) — unchanged from c7
Throop et al. 2020 RNAAS 4, 74 (doi.org/10.3847/2515-5172/ab9088) — unchanged from c7 (ISS-1991 fix landed in c8 cycle prior)

summary_for_decider: WIN-017 c8 verification: ISS-1998 patch verified PASS on origin/main. Verdict unchanged (Refuted by Data). The decider's split-and-strengthen execution exceeded the literal ISS description by converting a passive concession into active falsification — recommend this style for future readability splits where a falsification argument was buried in a parenthetical. No new substantive holes; same pipeline-bookkeeping note as WIN-028 c9 about race between verify_pending_sweep and universal-pusher rescue (7-second gap, retry would close it). Recommend no further work; pop queue_id 343.
